checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2019-02-24 value 633534000 vs end 2018-02-25 value 428469000
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2019-05-26 value 574389000 vs end 2018-05-27 value 342322000

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill end 2019-08-25 value 235630000 vs Goodwill end 2011-11-27 value 240970000
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end 2019-08-25 value 42794000 vs end 2010-11-28 value 84652000
OtherAssetsNoncurrent end 2019-08-25 value 134712000 vs end 2011-11-27 value 107997000

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss annual 2016-11-28 to 2017-11-26 value 281403000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities value 525941000

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet end 2019-08-25 value 935557000 vs end 2018-08-26 value 938253000
GrossProfit 2019-05-27 to 2019-08-25 value 766746000 on Revenue 1447081000 (~53% margin) vs GrossProfit 2018-05-28 to 2018-08-26 value 741562000 on Revenue 1394153000 (~53.2% margin)
evidence.location: GrossProfit/Revenue Q3 FY2019 vs Q3 FY2018

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: NetIncomeLoss, quarterly Q1 FY2018 (tax-reform related loss)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end 2019-02-24 value 8332000 on AccountsReceivableNetCurrent 633534000 (~1.3% coverage) vs end 2018-02-25 value 11282000 on AccountsReceivableNetCurrent 428469000 (~2.6% coverage)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly XBRL facts for Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, COGS, GrossProfit, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow statement items, receivables, inventory, and allowance accounts from FY2008/2010 through Q3 FY2019 (2019-08-25), filed across 10-K and 10-Q filings 2011-2019

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., channel stuffing to wholesale customers, bill-and-hold arrangements, or extended payment terms) causing accounts receivable to balloon far faster than reported sales, inflating reported revenue/receivable quality while true collectability lags.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2019-08-25 value 722001000 vs end 2018-08-26 value 487240000 (+48% YoY)
RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax quarterly 2019-05-27 to 2019-08-25 value 1447081000 vs SalesRevenueNet quarterly 2018-05-28 to 2018-08-26 value 1394153000 (+3.8% YoY)
rationale_evidence.location: Revenue, Q3 FY2019 vs Q3 FY2018

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2019-02-24 value 633534000 vs end 2018-02-25 value 428469000 (+47.9% YoY) while revenue grew only ~6.8% (Q1 FY2019 1434458000 vs Q1 FY2018 1343685000)
rationale_evidence.location: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent and Revenue, Q1 FY2019 vs Q1 FY2018

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad debt/allowance reserve growth did not keep pace with the sharp rise in gross receivables, resulting in a declining allowance-to-receivable coverage ratio; this understates the doubtful-accounts reserve relative to a rapidly expanding and potentially riskier receivable base, overstating net realizable receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=9438000 (2019-08-25) on AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=722001000 (~1.31% coverage) vs Allowance=9113000 (2018-08-26) on AR=487240000 (~1.87% coverage)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=8332000 (2019-02-24) on AR=633534000 (~1.3% coverage) vs Allowance=11282000 (2018-02-25) on AR=428469000 (~2.6% coverage)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Alternative non-fraud explanation: change in a receivables factoring/securitization or intercompany financing arrangement around the March 2019 IPO could have moved previously off-balance-sheet or sold receivables back onto the balance sheet, mechanically inflating reported AR without a corresponding revenue increase; this would represent a timing/classification shift rather than a true earnings-quality problem, but cannot be confirmed from the data provided.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent rose from 485485000 (2017-11-26, pre-IPO FY2017 10-K) to 722001000 (2019-08-25, post-IPO Q3 FY2019)

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew 47-68% YoY in Q1-Q3 FY2019 while revenue grew only 4-7% YoY, a stark and consistent divergence across three consecutive quarters

Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables declined markedly (from ~2.6% to ~1.3%) even as receivables surged, indicating reserve build did not keep pace with receivable growth

Other checklist areas (inventory/margin trends, NI vs OCF divergence, earnings smoothness, soft-asset growth) show no material red flags, and multiple 10-K/A amendments in 2015/2009 add minor chronology concern but are not conclusive

The AR anomaly coincides with the company's March 2019 IPO, which could reflect a benign change in receivable financing/factoring arrangements rather than misstatement, but this cannot be confirmed from the data provided
